commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Maintainer review is needed because the PR changes a documented security/runtime default and the contributor still needs real behavior proof that automation cannot provide for them.; Needs attention: The diff changes a security-sensitive filesystem helper default while leaving security guidance on the old default.; Review finding: [P2] Update the fs-safe security docs with the new default — src/infra/fs-safe-default... (sha=6a5aa688c74b4f7060a312730d0d8aed1a9ad5b9)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes OpenClaw's fs-safe Python default from off to auto, rewrites the default-mode test to subprocess checks, updates stale guardrail fixtures, and removes lint-only OpenRouter fallback spreads.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was run. Source inspection supports the reported failure path: current main configures fs-safe mode off, and fs-safe 0.2.6 throws helper-unavailable for POSIX pinned writes in that mode.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer review is needed because the PR changes a documented security/runtime default and the contributor still needs real behavior proof that automation cannot provide for them.

target.title: Patch/fs safe 0.2.6 integration
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `main` at `a134683` was failing CI after the `@openclaw/fs-safe@0.2.6` integration exposed POSIX pinned-write behavior and a non-isolated Vitest mock leak.
- Solution: Keep OpenClaw aligned with fs-safe 0.2.6’s viable `auto` helper mode while making the fs-safe defaults test run in subprocesses so it cannot mutate shared test state.
- What changed: Updated stale version guardrails, fixed status redaction test fixtures, removed OpenRouter lint-only spread fallbacks, and corrected Windows ACL test coverage for the current fs-safe contract.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No product feature behavior, gateway protocol, plugin API, exec/tool policy, or user-facing UI behavior beyond preserving fs-safe write viability.

## Motivation

- `main` CI was red on commit `a13468320c6` across build/test/lint lanes. The failures were blocking downstream PRs and obscuring unrelated changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `@openclaw/fs-safe@0.2.6` pinned writes work under OpenClaw’s default fs-safe config, and the fs-safe defaults test no longer poisons other non-isolated Vitest files.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Node 26.0.0, pnpm workspace installed, branch `patch/fs-safe-0.2.6-integration`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/fs-safe-defaults.test.ts src/commitments/runtime.test.ts src/commitments/store.test.ts src/commitments/extraction.test.ts src/commitments/commitments-full-chain.integration.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/fs-safe-compat.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts src/security/windows-acl.test.ts src/scripts/control-ui-i18n.test.ts test/scripts/root-package-overrides.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.redaction.test.ts src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/infra/fs-safe-defaults.test.ts src/infra/fs-safe-defaults.ts src/security/windows-acl.test.ts extensions/openrouter/provider-routing.ts --threads=8`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/fs-safe-defaults.test.ts src/infra/fs-safe-defaults.ts src/security/windows-acl.test.ts extensions/openrouter/provider-routing.ts scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts test/scripts/root-package-overrides.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.redaction.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - Vitest: `Test Files 16 passed (16)` / `Tests 284 passed (284)`
  - Oxlint: `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.`
  - tsgo core test config: exited `0`
  - tsgo extensions test config: exited `0`
  - oxfmt: `All matched files use the correct format.`
  - `git diff --check`: exited `0`
- Observed result after fix: The previously failing fs-safe, commitments, managed image, plugin SDK compatibility, status redaction, OpenRouter lint, and package-version guardrail checks pass locally.
- What was not tested: Full GitHub Actions matrix and live installed OpenClaw desktop/gateway runtime.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): GitHub Actions on `a13468320c6` failed with `FsSafeError: Python helper is required for pinned writes on this platform`, stale package-version assertions, status fixture type errors, and OpenRouter `unicorn(no-useless-fallback-in-spread)` lint failures.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `@openclaw/fs-safe@0.2.6` changed POSIX pinned-write behavior so `mode: "off"` no longer provides the needed write path. Separately, `src/infra/fs-safe-defaults.test.ts` mocked `@openclaw/fs-safe/config` in a non-isolated Vitest runner, which could leak into unrelated fs-safe tests.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The fs-safe defaults test asserted calls through a process-global mock instead of verifying effective runtime config in an isolated process.
- Contributing context (if known): Several guardrail tests also had stale expected versions after dependency bumps.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/fs-safe-defaults.test.ts`, commitments store/runtime tests, managed image attachment tests, `src/plugin-sdk/fs-safe-compat.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: OpenClaw’s fs-safe default config remains compatible with fs-safe 0.2.6 pinned writes and env overrides still win.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The defaults test now verifies effective config in a subprocess, avoiding shared Vitest module state while directly testing the process-global contract.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Commitments, managed image, and plugin SDK fs-safe compatibility tests exercise real write paths.
- If no new test is added, why not: Existing behavior tests already cover write-path integration; this PR fixes the defaults test isolation and updates stale expectations.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

OpenClaw now uses fs-safe Python `auto` mode by default when no fs-safe env override is set. Existing `FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE` and `OPENCLAW_FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE` overrides are preserved.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[non-isolated Vitest] -> [mock @openclaw/fs-safe/config] -> [fs-safe write tests see wrong config] -> [helper-unavailable]

After:
[fs-safe defaults test] -> [subprocess config check]
[other fs-safe tests] -> [real runtime config] -> [pinned writes succeed]
```
## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw checkout, Node 26.0.0, pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): Default fs-safe config with no `FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE` or `OPENCLAW_FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE` for the default-mode assertion.

### Steps

1. Check out `patch/fs-safe-0.2.6-integration`.
2. Run the focused Vitest command listed in Real behavior proof.
3. Run focused oxlint, tsgo, oxfmt, and `git diff --check`.

### Expected

- Focused tests, lint, type probes, and formatting checks pass.

### Actual

- Focused tests, lint, type probes, and formatting checks passed locally.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: fs-safe default-mode subprocess checks, env override checks, commitments write path, managed image write path, plugin SDK fs-safe compatibility, stale package-version guardrails, status redaction fixture typing, OpenRouter lint cleanup.
- Edge cases checked: `FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE=require`, `OPENCLAW_FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE=auto`, near-miss accented Windows SYSTEM principal remains untrusted.
- What you did **not** verify: Full remote CI matrix and live desktop/gateway app behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Yes
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: No migration. Operators who need a specific fs-safe helper mode can continue setting `FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE` or `OPENCLAW_FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Defaulting fs-safe to `auto` can start the optional Python helper where available.
  - Mitigation: This matches fs-safe 0.2.6’s viable pinned-write path, and explicit env overrides still take precedence.
- Risk: Subprocess-based defaults tests are slightly heavier than pure unit mocks.
  - Mitigation: The test is small and avoids leaking process-global mocks into non-isolated Vitest workers.
target.author: juanhuaxu
